prompt: The method is accidentally overloaded instead of overriding. What is the bug?
choices: Dog.speak(int) correctly overrides Animal.speak(String) because it has the same name
Dog.speak(int) has a different parameter type than Animal.speak(String), so it is an overload not an override; a.speak('Bark') dispatches to Animal.speak(String) and prints 'Bark'
Overloading is not allowed in subclasses
The compiler should reject Dog.speak(int) as it conflicts with the parent method signature
explanation: Overriding requires the same method signature (name + parameter types). Dog.speak(int) is a new overload, not an override. Animal.speak(String) is inherited unchanged. a.speak('Bark') calls Animal.speak(String) → 'Bark'. The @Override annotation would catch this mistake at compile time.
